children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: On or after the date that is 180 days after , the Secretary may establish a program, to be known as the “Department of Energy National Lab Jobs ACCESS Program”, under which the Secretary may award, on a competitive basis, 5-year grants to eligible entities described in subsection (c) for the Federal share of the costs of pre-apprenticeship programs and apprenticeship programs described in subsection (b).December 20, 2019

children.heading: Pre-apprenticeship and apprenticeship programs described
children.label: b Pre-apprenticeship and apprenticeship programs described

children.text: leads to recognized postsecondary credentials for secondary school and postsecondary students;

is focused on skills and qualifications needed, as determined by the Secretary in consultation with the directors of the National Laboratories, to meet the immediate and ongoing needs of traditional and emerging technician positions (including machinists and cybersecurity technicians) at the National Laboratories and covered facilities of the National Nuclear Security Administration;
is established in consultation with a National Laboratory or covered facility of the National Nuclear Security Administration;
is registered with and approved by the Secretary of Labor or a State apprenticeship agency; and
ensures that participants in the pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program do not displace paid employees.
demonstrates experience in implementing and providing career planning and career pathways toward pre-apprenticeship programs or apprenticeship programs;

children.label: A
children.text: has a relationship with a National Laboratory or covered facility of the National Nuclear Security Administration;

children.label: B
children.text: has knowledge of the technician workforce needs of the laboratory or facility and the associated security requirements of the laboratory or facility; and

children.label: C
children.text: is eligible to enter into an agreement with the laboratory or facility that would be paid for in part or entirely from grant funds received under this section;

demonstrates the ability to recruit and support individuals who plan to work in relevant technician positions upon the successful completion of the pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program;
provides students who complete the pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program with, or prepares such students for obtaining, a recognized postsecondary credential;

uses related instruction that is specifically aligned with the needs of the laboratory or facility and utilizes workplace learning advisors and on-the-job training to the greatest extent possible; and
demonstrates successful outcomes connecting graduates of the pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program to careers relevant to the program.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: d Applications
children.text: If the Secretary establishes the program described in subsection (a), an eligible entity described in subsection (c) seeking a grant under the program shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.
is a member of an industry or sector partnership;

children.label: A
children.text: a local educational agency, a secondary school, a provider of adult education, an area career and technical education school, or an institution of higher education (such as a community college) that includes basic science, technology, and mathematics education in the related instruction; or

children.label: B
children.text: an apprenticeship program that was registered with the Department of Labor or a State apprenticeship agency before the date on which the eligible entity applies for the grant under subsection (d);

works with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, or veterans organizations to transition members of the Armed Forces and veterans to pre-apprenticeship programs or apprenticeship programs in a relevant sector;
plans to use the grant to carry out the pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program with an entity that receives State funding or is operated by a State agency; and

children.label: A
children.text: young adults ages 16 to 29, inclusive; or

children.label: B
children.text: individuals with barriers to employment.

children.heading: Additional consideration
children.label: f Additional consideration
children.text: In making grants under this section, the Secretary may consider regional diversity.

children.heading: Limitation on applications
children.label: g Limitation on applications
children.text: An eligible entity described in subsection (c) may not submit, either individually or as part of a joint application, more than one application for a grant under this section during any one fiscal year.

children.heading: Limitations on amount of grant
children.label: h Limitations on amount of grant
children.text: The amount of a grant provided under this section may not, for any 24-month period of the 5-year grant period, exceed $500,000.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: i Non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program carried out using a grant under this section shall be not less than 25 percent of the total cost of the program.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: j Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance to eligible entities described in subsection (c) to leverage the existing job training and education programs of the Department of Labor and other relevant programs at appropriate Federal agencies.

children.label: i
children.text: any entity that receives a grant under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: any activity carried out using a grant under this section; and

children.label: iii
children.text: best practices used to leverage the investment of the Federal Government under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: an assessment of the results achieved by the program, including the rate of employment for participants after completing a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program carried out using a grant under this section.

children.heading: Performance reports
children.label: 2 Performance reports
children.text: Not later than one year after the establishment of a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program using a grant awarded under this section, and annually thereafter, the entity carrying out the program shall submit to the Secretary and the Secretary of Labor a report on the effectiveness of the program based on the accountability measures described in clauses (i) and (ii) of .section 3141(b)(2)(A) of title 29

children.heading: ESEA terms
children.label: 1 ESEA terms
children.text: The terms “local educational agency” and “secondary school” have the meanings given the terms in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: WIOA terms
children.label: 2 WIOA terms
children.text: The terms “career planning”, “community-based organization”, “customized training”, “economic development agency”, “individual with a barrier to employment”, “industry or sector partnership”, “on-the-job training”, “recognized postsecondary credential”, and “workplace learning advisor” have the meanings given such terms in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Apprenticeship program
children.label: 3 Apprenticeship program
children.text: The term “apprenticeship program” means a program registered under the Act of (commonly known as the “National Apprenticeship Act”; , chapter 663; et seq.).August 16, 193750 Stat. 66429 U.S.C. 50

children.heading: Area career and technical education school
children.label: 4 Area career and technical education school
children.text: The term “area career and technical education school” has the meaning given the term in .section 2302 of title 20

children.heading: Community college
children.label: 5 Community college
children.text: The term “community college” has the meaning given the term “junior or community college” in .section 1058(f) of title 20

children.heading: Covered facility of the national nuclear security administration
children.label: 6 Covered facility of the national nuclear security administration
children.text: The term “covered facility of the National Nuclear Security Administration” means a national security laboratory or a nuclear weapons production facility as such terms are defined in section 2501 of title 50.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: an industry or sector partnership;

children.label: ii
children.text: an employer or industry association;

children.label: iii
children.text: a labor-management organization;

children.label: iv
children.text: a local workforce development board or State workforce development board;

children.label: v
children.text: a 2- or 4-year institution of higher education that offers an educational program leading to an associate’s or bachelor’s degree in conjunction with a certificate of completion of apprenticeship;

children.label: vi
children.text: the Armed Forces (including the National Guard and Reserves);

children.label: vii
children.text: a community-based organization; or

children.label: viii
children.text: an economic development agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a pre-apprenticeship program, is a local educational agency, a secondary school, an area career and technical education school, a provider of adult education, a State workforce development board, a local workforce development board, or a community-based organization, that administers the program with any required coordination and necessary approvals from the Secretary of Labor or a State department of labor.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 8 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Local workforce development board
children.label: 9 Local workforce development board
children.text: The term “local workforce development board” has the meaning given the term “local board” in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: 10 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.heading: Nonprofit organization
children.label: 11 Nonprofit organization
children.text: The term “nonprofit organization” means an organization that is described in and exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such title.section 501(c) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: designed to prepare individuals to enter and succeed in an apprenticeship program; and

children.label: B
children.text: that has a documented partnership with at least one, if not more, apprenticeship programs.

children.heading: Provider of adult education
children.label: 13 Provider of adult education
children.text: The term “provider of adult education” has the meaning given the term “eligible provider” in .section 3272 of title 29

children.heading: Related instruction
children.label: 14 Related instruction
children.text: The term “related instruction” means an organized and systematic form of instruction designed to provide an individual in a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program with the knowledge of the technical subjects related to the intended occupation of the individual after completion of the program.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 15 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor, except as otherwise specified in this section.

children.heading: Sponsor
children.label: 16 Sponsor
children.text: The term “sponsor” means any person, association, committee, or organization operating a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program and in whose name the program is (or is to be) registered or approved.

children.heading: State apprenticeship agency
children.label: 17 State apprenticeship agency
children.text: The term “State apprenticeship agency” has the meaning given that term in section 29.2 of title 29, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: State workforce development board
children.label: 18 State workforce development board
children.text: The term “State workforce development board” has the meaning given the term “State board” in .section 3102 of title 29

children.label: i
children.text: proactively addresses workforce needs using a dual customer approach, which considers the needs of both employees and employers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has partnered with a sponsor of a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program or is a sponsor of a pre-apprenticeship program or apprenticeship program; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include a community organization, an employer organization, a community college, a temporary staffing agency, a State workforce development board, a local workforce development board, or a labor or labor-management organization.
